title: Header: remove sticky behavior and center/clarify logo

requirements.summary: Remove sticky/fixed positioning so the header (including its mobile sub-rows) scrolls with the page content.
acceptanceCriteria: When scrolling any page, the header scrolls away with the page content (it is not sticky/fixed).
No header sub-sections (desktop row, mobile search row, mobile nav row) remain stuck to the viewport during scroll.

file_operations.description: Remove the sticky positioning behavior from the root <header> element (e.g., eliminate Tailwind classes that cause sticky/fixed placement such as "sticky"/"top-0") and ensure no other header sub-rows rely on viewport-stuck positioning, so the entire header participates in normal document flow while retaining existing border/background styling.

requirements.summary: Center the header logo on desktop and mobile and improve its visibility while keeping the existing image-fallback-to-text behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop widths, the logo appears centered within the header area rather than grouped to the left next to the menu button.
On mobile widths, the logo remains centered in the header area without overlapping other controls.
The logo is visibly clearer than before (increased size and/or contrast), and the text fallback ("SPEAKR") still renders if the image fails to load.

file_operations.description: Restructure the top header row layout so the logo link is centered horizontally (desktop + mobile) independent of left menu and right actions (e.g., use a relative container with an absolutely centered logo region or a 3-column grid). Increase logo clarity by adjusting the image styling (larger height than the current h-10 and stronger contrast such as adding a Tailwind drop-shadow/contrast utility) and update the fallback text styling for comparable prominence. Preserve the existing onError-driven fallback behavior and continue using the same logo asset path: frontend/public/assets/generated/speakr-logo-reupload.dim_1536x864.png.
